description:
You are the site administrator of a small self-hosted web application.  
    All application users are stored in a plain-text configuration file located at  

        /home/user/site-config/users.conf  

    The file already exists and looks exactly like this (four lines, each terminated with a single Unix newline “\n”):  

        alice:admin
        bob:editor
        charlie:viewer
        diana:viewer

    Each record is “username:role”.  
    An account is considered “active” when the line is present in the file.  
    To deactivate an account you must completely remove that line (not comment it out).

    Required changes
    1. Add the new user “alex” with the role “editor”.
    2. Deactivate (i.e. delete) the user “bob”.
    3. Leave every other line untouched and keep the original ordering for the remaining lines.
       After all changes, the file must contain exactly four lines in this exact order:  

           alice:admin
           charlie:viewer
           diana:viewer
           alex:editor

    Verification log
    After you finish editing the configuration file, create the log file  

        /home/user/site-config/user_update.log  

    with the following single line (terminated by a Unix newline):

        active_user_count=4

    The automated test will verify:
    • /home/user/site-config/users.conf exists and matches the four-line content shown above byte-for-byte.  
    • /home/user/site-config/user_update.log exists and contains exactly the text “active_user_count=4\n”.  

    Perform whatever commands you need (likely no more than 3-4 commands) to achieve this final state.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts:
    • Directory /home/user/site-config/ already exists and is writable by the current user.
    • File /home/user/site-config/users.conf exists with the initial four lines:
          alice:admin
          bob:editor
          charlie:viewer
          diana:viewer
      (each line ends with “\n”, no blank trailing line).
    • File /home/user/site-config/user_update.log does NOT exist.

    Expected final state after successful completion:
    • /home/user/site-config/users.conf content (including exact newline placement):

          alice:admin\n
          charlie:viewer\n
          diana:viewer\n
          alex:editor\n

      Total size 60 bytes.

    • /home/user/site-config/user_update.log content:

          active_user_count=4\n

      Total size 21 bytes.
